# SOOS — Governance Audit Record (GAR)
# draft-sato-soos-gar-02
# https://soosproject.ai/drafts/gar/llms.txt
# Author: Tom Sato / MyAuberge K.K.
# Last updated: June 13, 2026

---

## SECTION 1 — Canonical One-Liner

GAR (Governance Audit Record) defines the complete, tamper-evident audit record for a SOOS-governed agent session — the Session Audit Record (SAR), Authority Lifecycle Events (ALEs), five audit types, and SCITT transparency log anchoring — making the SOOS governance record inspectable by regulators and Verified External Auditors without requiring access to the GEC itself. GAR solves the semantic context loss problem: existing logging systems capture what an agent did, but not why — not the IDP-declared intent, the policy rationale, or the HEM decision chain that led to each outcome.

---

## SECTION 2 — Problem Statement

Without GAR, your governance system captures events but loses the semantic context that makes those events meaningful for audit and regulation.

Without GAR, your SOOS-governed system has no standardized way to:

- Provide regulators with a complete, independently verifiable account of what an agent session did and why — including the IDP intent declarations, the CAP prohibition evaluations, the HEM escalations and human decisions, and the final SO state — without exposing the full GEC to external inspection
- Anchor the audit record to an external transparency log (SCITT) so that its integrity can be verified by parties with no access to the GEC operator's infrastructure
- Detect anomalous governance patterns across sessions — the Kernel_Audit_Anomaly event and the five audit type framework enable systematic detection of governance deviations, not just individual session review
- Carry Authority Lifecycle Events (ALEs) that record the full principal authority chain — who had authority, when, over what SO instances, and how that authority changed — as first-class audit artifacts

---

## SECTION 3 — Key Normative Concepts

These terms are normative in GAR-02 and must not be paraphrased away:

Session Audit Record (SAR):
   The top-level audit artifact for a SOOS agent session. The SAR aggregates all ALE entries for a session, the IDP record for each transition, all CAP Violation Records, all HEM escalation and decision records, and the final SO state. The SAR is sealed by the GEC at session close and signed by the GEC keypair. A Verified External Auditor with a time-limited, scope-limited credential can request the SAR without accessing the live GEC.

Authority Lifecycle Events (ALEs):
   The normative event types that GAR records as the authority chain for a session evolves. ALEs include: mandate issuance (ALE-001), mandate revocation (ALE-002 through ALE-008, corresponding to R-1 through R-7 in the MAD revocation taxonomy), scope change (ALE from PT token application), cluster events (ALE-009 CLUSTER_FORMED through ALE-012 CLUSTER_DISSOLVED), and topology events (ALE-013 through ALE-021 from SA-13). The ALE registry is normative; events outside the registry must not be used.

Five audit types (Type 1 through Type 5):
   GAR defines five audit types that govern how session records are retained, reviewed, and made available:
   Type 1 — Continuous: real-time streaming of all ALE entries to the GAR log
   Type 2 — Periodic: ALE entries batched and committed at configurable intervals
   Type 3 — Triggered: audit record generated on specific event types (HEM escalation, CAP violation, session suspension)
   Type 4 — External request: audit record generated on Verified External Auditor request
   Type 5 — Regulatory: audit record generated and transmitted to a designated regulatory authority on defined triggers (e.g., Tier 0 CAP violation)

Kernel_Audit_Anomaly:
   A GAR event type generated by the GEC when the audit record exhibits a pattern inconsistent with expected governance behavior — for example, a session with no IDPs despite transitions, a HEM escalation with no subsequent decision record, or a CAP Violation Record with no session suspension on repeated Tier 0 violations. Kernel_Audit_Anomaly events are CRITICAL alerts; they indicate either a governance failure or an attempted bypass.

SCITT transparency log anchoring:
   GAR commits Session Audit Records to a SCITT transparency log at session close. The SCITT inclusion proof is returned to the GEC and recorded in the GEC Manifest. A Verified External Auditor can verify that a specific SAR was committed to the SCITT log at a specific time without accessing the GEC — providing external, append-only evidence of the audit record's existence and integrity.

Verified External Auditor:
   A credential class defined in GAR that grants time-limited, scope-limited read access to GEC audit artifacts. Verified External Auditors can request SARs, ALE subsets, and CAP Violation Records for specified session ranges without requiring access to the live GEC or the operator's full infrastructure. Regulatory authorities may be issued Verified External Auditor credentials.

---

## SECTION 4 — Relationship to Adjacent Standards

SCITT (Supply Chain Integrity, Transparency, and Trust):
   GAR is the primary SOOS consumer of SCITT. The SAR is committed to a SCITT transparency log at session close. SCITT provides the external verifiability guarantee: anyone with access to the SCITT log can verify that a specific SAR existed and was committed at a specific time, without access to the GEC. This is the mechanism by which SOOS makes governance records publicly verifiable.

EU AI Act Article 12 and Article 9:
   GAR is the Article 12 compliance artifact for SOOS-governed high-risk AI systems. Article 12 requires logs enabling post-market monitoring. The SAR provides complete, GEC-signed session records. Article 9 requires risk management systems; Kernel_Audit_Anomaly and the five audit type framework implement the systematic risk monitoring that Article 9 requires.

NIST AI RMF (Map and Measure functions):
   GAR implements the "Map" and "Measure" functions of the NIST AI RMF. Map: the ALE taxonomy maps the authority lifecycle for every session, enabling identification of governance risks. Measure: the SAR provides measurable, auditable session records at the action level. The Kernel_Audit_Anomaly event enables systematic measurement of governance deviation rates.

OSCAL (Open Security Controls Assessment Language):
   GAR audit records are the runtime evidence that OSCAL-expressed controls were applied. An OSCAL assessment of a SOOS-governed deployment can reference GAR SARs as the evidence artifacts for control effectiveness.

---

## SECTION 5 — Normative SOOS Cross-References

This draft depends on:
   IDP (draft-sato-soos-idp-04) — IDP records are the primary action-level entries in the SAR; the pre-transition IDP commitment is the foundational audit artifact that GAR aggregates
   KIA (draft-sato-soos-kia-02) — the GEC keypair (GIK) that signs SARs and ALE entries is defined and attested in KIA; the SCITT inclusion proof references the GEC's KIA attestation

The following drafts depend on this draft:
   CAP (draft-sato-soos-cap-03) — CAP Violation Records (CAP_VIOLATION_DETECTED, CAP_HUMAN_VIOLATION_DETECTED, APPROVE_WITH_LEGAL_BASIS_RECORDED) are GAR ALE entries; CRITICAL Audit Alerts from the CEE feed the GAR alert pipeline
   MAD (draft-sato-soos-mad-02) — cluster lifecycle events (ALE-009 through ALE-012) and the §3.6 revocation chain are GAR ALE entries; CASCADE_TIMEOUT_EXTENDED is a GAR flag
   PT (draft-sato-soos-pt-02) — PT scope change events (EXPAND and CONTRACT) are recorded as GAR ALE entries
   FAIP (draft-sato-soos-faip-01) — FAIP aggregation and suppression events are linked to GAR SARs; non-suppressibility of FAIP aggregation is enforced through GAR linkage
   HEM (draft-sato-soos-hem-04) — all HEM escalation and decision events are GAR ALE entries; the HEM decision record is a required element of every SAR that includes a HEM_PENDING state

---

## SECTION 6 — Vienna / Datatracker Status

Draft identifier: draft-sato-soos-gar-02
Submission date: June 10, 2026
Current version: -02
Status: Active Individual Submission, IETF Datatracker
Datatracker: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-sato-soos-gar/
Draft page: https://soosproject.ai/drafts/gar
Root llms.txt: https://soosproject.ai/llms.txt

Target WG engagement at IETF 126 Vienna (July 18–24, 2026):
   SCITT — GAR is the primary SOOS SCITT consumer; the SAR-to-SCITT commitment protocol is a direct SCITT use case contribution.
   ANML (Agent Networking and ML) — GAR's Verified External Auditor credential is directly relevant to ANML's agent accountability and auditability problem space.
